data.section_desc: Note: Hands-on Labs are an essential part of the learning experience; however, access to them is not included with the video course subscription. A separate Labs subscription is required.

data.video_name: Amazon EC2 Fundamentals: Purchasing Options, Saving plans and Tenancy
2.3 Java Basics - Create executable Java applications with a main method
data.video_description: 2.3 Java Basics - Create executable Java applications with a main method
data.lab_name: Introduction to Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2)
data.lab_description:
1.This lab walks you through the steps to launch and configure a virtual machine in the Amazon cloud.

2.You will practice using Amazon Machine Images to launch Amazon EC2 Instances and use key pairs for SSH authentication to log into your instance. You will create a web page and publish it.

data.video_name: Difference between Public, Private and Elastic IP address

data.lab_name: Creating an application load balancer from AWS CLI
data.lab_description:
1. This lab walks you through the steps to Create an Application Load Balancer from AWS CLI

  2. You will practice it using AWS EC2 and AWS Load Balancing

data.lab_name: Creating an Application Load Balancer and Auto Scaling Group in AWS
data.lab_description:
1. This lab walks you through the steps to create and configure an Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling with Launch templates using the AWS Management Console. 

  2. You will practice using Amazon Machine Image (AMI) to launch Amazon EC2 Instances using Launch templates and will use key pairs for SSH authentication to log into your instance.

  3. You will create a web page and publish it.

  4. Duration: 55 minutes

  5. AWS Region: US East (N. Virginia)

data.video_name: 7.6 Working with Methods and Encapsulation - Apply access modifiers
data.video_description: 7.6 Working with Methods and Encapsulation - Apply access modifiers
data.lab_name: Creating and configuring a network load balancer in AWS
data.lab_description: This lab walks you through the steps to create and configure a network load balancer.

data.video_name: AWS Gateway Load Balancer - Monitoring and Pricing
Amazon Simple Storage Service: Versioning & Features
S3 ACLs and Bucket Policy - Demo

data.lab_name: Creating S3 Lifecycle Policy
data.lab_description: This Lab walks you through the steps on how to Create a Lifecycle Rule for an Object in S3 Bucket.

Amazon S3: Encryption - How it Works?
Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) Overview
Amazon Elastic Block Storage: Data Protection and Access Control
Amazon Elastic Block Storage: Snapshots - Demo
Amazon EBS: Creating, Attaching and Detaching Volume
Amazon Elastic Block Storage: TroubleShooting Common Issues
AWS IAM: Users, Groups, Policies & MFA
IAM Users and IAM Groups - Demo

data.lab_name: Introduction to Amazon GuardDuty
data.lab_description:
1. This lab walks you through the steps to Enable GuardDuty and create Sample findings.

  2. You will practice using Amazon GuardDuty.

data.video_name: Amazon Macie Hands on - Create Macie job
Amazon Macie Hands on - Macie job run and Findings

data.lab_name: Discover sensitive data present in S3 bucket using Amazon Macie
Deploying Amazon RDS Multi-AZ and Read Replica, Simulate Failover
data.lab_description: This lab walks you through the steps to launch the Amazon Aurora RDS DB instance with multi-AZ enabled and simulate failover.

data.video_name: Amazon Aurora Serverless V1 vs V2

data.lab_name: Create Redis cluster using ElastiCache
data.lab_description:
1. This lab walks you through the steps to launch a Redis cluster using ElastiCache through AWS Management Console. 

  2. You will query basic commands on Redis cluster after SSHing that with EC2 instance.

  3. Duration: 1 hour

  4. AWS Region: US East (N. Virginia)

data.lab_name: Using CloudWatch for Resource Monitoring, Create CloudWatch Alarms and Dashboards
data.lab_description: This lab walks you through the various CloudWatch features available which are used for resource monitoring.

data.video_name: AWS CloudWatch Logs Agent, Unified Agent

data.lab_name: Check Compliance status of S3 Bucket using AWS Config
data.lab_description:
1. This lab walks you through the steps to create SNS topics and subscriptions. You will set up the AWS Config and create Config Rules. 

  2. You will practice using the CloudWatch event and Lambda function to get notified about any changes through email.

  3. Duration: 90 minutes

  4. AWS Region: US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1

data.video_name: Create AWS Config and SNS Topic: Hands - on
AWS Config: Monitor the Compliance Status

data.lab_name: Auditing Resource Compliance with AWS config
data.lab_description:
1. This lab walks you through the steps to deploy Auditing Resource Compliance with AWS config

  2. You will practice it using AWS config

data.lab_name: Introduction to Amazon CloudFormation
data.lab_description: This lab walks you through to AWS CloudFormation features. In this lab, we will demonstrate the use AWS CloudFormation Stack in creating a simple LAMP Server.

data.lab_name: Build Amazon VPC with Public and Private Subnets from Scratch
data.lab_description:
Objective

  1. Learn how to build Public and Private subnets from scratch.

  2. VPC wizard will not be used. So every component required to build public and private subnets will be created and configured manually.

  3. This will give an in-depth understanding of internal components of VPC and subnets.

Lab Details:    

  1. This lab walks you through to AWS Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) creation from scratch. 

  2. In this lab, we will create a VPC without using VPC Wizard. We will create a public and private subnets. Along with that we will be configuring Route tables.

data.video_name: AWS VPC Overview: Security Groups & Network ACLs
4.3 Develop code that uses binary versions of functional interfaces
data.lab_name: Creating AWS VPC Flow Logs and Generating Traffic
data.lab_description:
1. This lab walks you through the steps to Creating VPC and VPC Flow Logs.

  2. You will Practice VPC Flow Logs with VPC

data.lab_name: Creating NAT Gateways in AWS
data.lab_description: This lab walks you through the steps to Create A NAT Gateway and allow internet access to Instance in Private Subnet.

data.video_name: 4.2 Develop code that uses primitive versions of functional interfaces
data.lab_name: Peer VPC with Transit Gateway and its components
data.lab_description:
1. This lab walks you through how to peer VPC with Transit Gateway. You will be creating 2 VPC with a public and private subnet. Launch EC2 Instances in both of the VPC and establish peering between them.

  2. You will be using the Transit gateway attachment to add VPC and add the entry in the route table. Then you will use SSH into the private EC2 from the public EC2 instance.

  3. Duration: 90 minutes

  4. AWS Region: US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1

data.video_name: Amazon CloudFront and Edge locations Overview
Amazon CloudFront and Edge Locations - Working TTL and Pricing

data.lab_name: Introduction to Amazon CloudFront
data.lab_description: This lab walks you through Amazon CloudFront creation and working. In this lab, you will create an Amazon CloudFront distribution. It will distribute a publicly accessible image file stored in an Amazon S3 bucket.

data.video_name: S3 Presigned Url vs CloudFront Signed Url
3rd Party Domain Integration with Route 53 & Cleanup
11.2 Identify the components required to connect to a database using the DriverManager class including the JDBC URL
data.lab_name: Transfer data to S3 using Amazon Kinesis Firehose
data.lab_description:
1. This lab walks you through the steps to create Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose delivery streams.

  2. You will practice using sample data present in the firehose, by sending them to Amazon S3 Bucket through  Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose delivery stream.

  3. Duration: 45 minutes

  4. AWS Region: US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1

data.video_name: Perform ETL with AWS Glue - Create Glue Crawler
Run Glue Crawler & Create Glue Job
Validate the Output from Glue Job

data.lab_name: Perform ETL operation in Glue with S3
data.lab_description:
1. This lab walks you through the steps to perform ETL operation in AWS Glue with Amazon S3 as a data source.

  2. You will practice using AWS Glue crawler to create a reference table in the Glue Data catalog's Databases and ETL Jobs to perform aggregation on top of tables present in the Glue Data catalog.

  3. Duration: 60 minutes

  4. AWS Region: US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1

data.video_name: Introduction to Simple Queuing Service (SQS) - Demo
Creating a SNS Topic and Subscribing through Email - Demo
Amazon ECS - Creating an ECS Cluster
Amazon ECS - Creating Services and Tasks

data.lab_name: Create a cluster in Amazon EKS and install kubectl
data.lab_description:
1. This lab walks you through the steps to create an EKS Cluster and understand each of its components.

  2. You will practice using the AWS Elastic Kubernetes Service cluster and install kubectl to get the cluster IP.

  3. Duration: 60 minutes

  4. AWS Region: US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1

data.lab_name: Create a Docker container using Dockerfile and store the image in ECR
data.lab_description:
1. This lab walks you through the steps to create an ECS Cluster with t2.micro instance type as container instance.

  2. You will practice using Amazon Elastic Container Registry's repository to push a custom docker image created using Dockerfile. You will also learn how to push the image to the ECR repository.

  3. Duration: 60 minutes

  4. AWS Region: US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1
